summary: A US‑Iran agreement was announced, prompting Israel to reaffirm its refusal to withdraw from southern Lebanon. Hezbollah launched another rocket barrage at Israeli forces in southern Lebanon, and a drone strike killed a driver in a vehicle there. Israeli forces continued targeted killings of Hamas commanders in Gaza.
key_points: The United States and Iran announced a signed agreement in Switzerland, with claims it includes Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon (Source: @inon_yttach/8999).
Israel's Defense Minister Katz stated Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon despite the US‑Iran ceasefire (Source: @manniefabian/58603).
Hezbollah fired a rocket barrage at Israeli troops advancing in southern Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194200).
A Lebanese security source reported an Israeli drone attack killed a driver in south Lebanon, the first lethal Israeli strike there since the US‑Iran agreement (Source: @VahidOnline/76373).
The IDF reported killing two Hamas operatives, a Nuseirat Battalion commander and a West Jabalia Battalion commander, in precise strikes in Gaza (Source: @manniefabian/58606).
US officials indicated the focus will shift to de‑mining the Strait of Hormuz after the agreement (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3994).
Iranian officials confirmed the existence of the agreement and discussed a 60‑day navigation period in the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @inon_yttach/8996).

sections.executive_summary: A US‑Iran agreement was signed in Switzerland, with claims it includes Israeli pull‑back from Lebanon, though Israel publicly rejected any withdrawal. Hezbollah responded with a rocket barrage against Israeli troops in southern Lebanon, and an Israeli drone strike killed a driver in south Lebanon. Israeli forces continued precision strikes against Hamas commanders in Gaza.

points: Iran and the United States announced a signed agreement in Switzerland, with reports that it includes Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon and a 60‑day navigation period in the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @inon_yttach/8999).
Iranian officials confirmed the agreement and discussed that Iran will control maritime services in the Strait of Hormuz after a 60‑day fee‑free period (Source: @asafroz/50341).

Iran’s deputy foreign minister said the 60‑day negotiation period will start only after frozen Iranian assets are released, and that nuclear talks are on hold until U.S. commitments are met (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3973).
A report indicated Iran will receive a $300 billion reconstruction fund contingent on compliance with the agreement (Source: @kianmeli1/88005).
Israel’s Defense Minister Katz declared Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon, Syria, or Gaza, and will maintain security zones indefinitely (Source: @manniefabian/58603).
The IDF reported killing two Hamas operatives, a Nuseirat Battalion commander and a West Jabalia Battalion commander, in precise strikes in Gaza (Source: @manniefabian/58606).
Israel’s Home Front Command lifted nationwide gathering limits up to 5,000 people, while maintaining stricter limits on the northern border (Source: @manniefabian/58604).

A senior U.S. official stated that an Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon is not a condition of the emerging US‑Iran agreement, but Israel retains the right to self‑defence against Hezbollah (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4012).
The IDF killed two Hamas cell commanders in Gaza, one from the Nuseirat Battalion and another from the West Jabalia Battalion, citing imminent threats (Source: @manniefabian/58606).
No specific West Bank incidents were mentioned in the source material.
Hezbollah fired a rocket barrage at Israeli troops advancing in southern Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194200).
An Israeli drone strike in south Lebanon killed a driver, marking the first lethal Israeli strike there since the US‑Iran agreement (Source: @VahidOnline/76373).
Israeli forces fired at residents attempting to return to the village of Tabnit in south Lebanon (Source: @lelotsenzura/93017).
A report from New_security8200 noted a strike in the town of Aaita al-Jabal in southern Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/201901).
No explicit operational details about Syria or Iraq were present in the source messages.
U.S. officials said de‑mining the Strait of Hormuz will become a priority after the US‑Iran agreement is signed (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3994).
Iranian statements indicated future control over maritime services in the Strait of Hormuz, affecting Gulf navigation (Source: @asafroz/50341).

key_developments: US and Iran signed an agreement in Switzerland, reportedly including Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon and a 60‑day navigation period in the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @inon_yttach/8999).
Israel’s Defense Minister Katz affirmed Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon, maintaining security zones (Source: @manniefabian/58603).
Hezbollah launched a rocket barrage against Israeli troops in southern Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194200).
An Israeli drone strike in south Lebanon killed a driver, the first lethal strike there since the US‑Iran deal (Source: @VahidOnline/76373).
IDF precision strikes eliminated two Hamas commanders in Gaza’s Nuseirat and West Jabalia battalions (Source: @manniefabian/58606).
U.S. plans to shift de‑mining efforts to the Strait of Hormuz following the agreement (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3994).
Iran announced it will control maritime services in the Strait of Hormuz after a 60‑day fee‑free period (Source: @asafroz/50341).
sections.threat_assessment: The US‑Iran agreement introduces a new diplomatic dimension but does not halt hostilities on the ground. Hezbollah’s continued rocket attacks and Israeli drone strikes in Lebanon indicate a high risk of escalation along the Israel‑Lebanon frontier. Israeli precision operations against Hamas leaders in Gaza demonstrate sustained offensive capability, while Iran’s control over Strait of Hormuz navigation could affect regional energy security.
sections.regional_response: Israel publicly rejected any conditional withdrawal from Lebanon, reinforcing its security zone policy. Lebanese officials expressed support for the agreement’s Lebanon provisions, while Hezbollah’s actions suggest it will test Israeli resolve. Gulf states are monitoring the Strait of Hormuz developments, with the U.S. planning de‑mining operations that could involve multinational coordination.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iranian asset releases and the implementation timeline of the 60‑day navigation period is critical for assessing maritime security. Continued surveillance of Hezbollah rocket launch sites and Israeli drone activity in south Lebanon is required to anticipate further clashes. Tracking Israeli strike patterns in Gaza will inform Hamas operational adjustments.
